text:
TD-PTP method. In the initial phase, historical trajectories of multiple pedestrian agents
are meticulously constructed and input into the encoder. The encoder processes this information to derive a low-dimensional latent space
representation. Notably, a controlled element of randomness is introduced to account for uncertainties during sample generation. Subsequently, the
trajectory data undergo encoding, transforming it into a condensed latent representation characterized by reduced dimensions, effectively capturing
salient features. In the final stage, a decoder network maps the latent space representation, typically generated by the encoder, back into the
original data space and the decoding process yields the predicted trajectories.
